content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 31, 2004

Joseph R. Brown, Jr.
President
Midland Management Corp.
23151 Moulton Parkway
Laguna Hills, CA 92653

Re: Your Request for Information
Our File No. G-04-181

Dear Mr. Brown:

This letter is in response to your request for general information regarding the statement of economic interests provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

During our telephone conversation on August 25, 2004, you indicated that you are a city council member required to complete the Form 700 statement of economic interests. You are not seeking written advice, but simply wish to propose that "managed" stock investment funds can be treated in the same way that mutual funds are treated for purposes of disclosure under the Act.

As we discussed, section 82034 defines "investment" to specifically exclude any "interest in a diversified mutual fund registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission under the Investment Company Act of 1940...." In order to expand this exception to include similar types of managed investment accounts, a legislative amendment to section 82034 would be needed.

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

CW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\04-181
